assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 70-78 detail all 5 criteria individually marked PASS: (1) Single license filter, (2) Comma-separated license filter, (3) Invalid license returns 400, (4) Pagination integration, (5) Response shape unchanged. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in the diff match the task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created)'. Lines 26-33 provide the file-by-file comparison table showing list.rs (modify/Modified/Match), service/mod.rs (modify/Modified/Match), and tests/api/package.rs (create/New file/Match). Line 33: 'No extra files are present in the diff beyond what the task specifies. All task-required files are present. The scope is fully contained.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created'. The report does not mention creating any sub-tasks anywhere in its content.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The report content exclusively consists of analysis, verification verdicts, and findings. No output file indicates any code modification was performed. All analysis references code via quotation (e.g., code blocks in criterion files showing existing code), not via edits.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings in a summary table and detailed domain findings sections but does not contain any merge action, merge command, or auto-merge instruction. The report ends with 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' (line 97), indicating it is a review comment for human consumption. The overall verdict is 'Overall: PASS' (line 17) which is a recommendation, not an action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, .env references, or credentials found in any added lines'. Lines 52-59 detail the scan categories: hardcoded passwords/secrets (None), API keys/tokens (None), private keys/certificates (None), .env references (None), cloud credentials (None), database connection strings (None). Line 60: 'The added code consists entirely of Rust application logic...No sensitive patterns detected.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` with 4 new test functions; no tests removed or weakened'. Lines 93-94 elaborate: '`tests/api/package.rs` is an entirely new file containing 4 new test functions and 80 lines of test code. No existing tests were modified, removed, or weakened. This is a purely additive change to the test suite.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all expected verdicts: Review Feedback (N/A), Root-Cause Investigation (N/A), Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (WARN), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). Domain findings section (lines 19-95) organizes these under four domains: Intent Alignment (Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), Security (Sensitive Pattern Scan), Correctness (CI Status, Acceptance Criteria), Style/Conventions (Test Quality with sub-components Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality; and Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: Lines 26-33 contain a file-by-file table comparing task specification to PR diff with Match status for each file. Lines 37-42 provide per-file line counts. Security: Lines 52-59 list each scanned pattern category with individual results (None found for each). Correctness: Lines 70-78 provide per-criterion code-level verification with specific function names, code constructs, and test names cited for each of the 5 acceptance criteria. Style/Conventions: Lines 83-94 assess repetitive test detection (4 distinct scenarios identified), test documentation (quotes all 4 doc comments verbatim), and test change classification (file name, function count, line count).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests that should be parameterized. Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 90: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews exist)'. Test Quality is marked PASS, indicating Eval Quality's N/A status does not negatively affect the combined verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 90: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews exist)'). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the report or any output file. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created' confirms no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created'. These appear in the summary table (lines 4-16) before the Domain Findings section (line 19+). The Domain Findings section covers only four domains (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) and does not include Review Feedback or Root-Cause Investigation, confirming these are determined independently from domain sub-agent analysis.
